description_long: This is a robust, industrial-style red corded phone with a rectangular metal body and a prominent, matching red handset. The handset is attached to the base by a coiled cord, and the phone features visible wear and chipping on its painted surface, suggesting heavy use or exposure to the elements. The front panel includes two large, square buttons—one red and one orange—likely for call initiation or emergency use, and the handset rests securely in a molded cradle. The sides of the phone are reinforced with angular flanges, adding to its durability and giving it a utilitarian appearance. The back is flat and unadorned, likely designed for wall mounting in public or industrial environments. The overall construction and finish indicate it is built to withstand rough handling and outdoor conditions.
description: A rugged, red metal corded phone with a coiled cord, large buttons, and visible wear, designed for public or industrial use. The handset rests in a sturdy cradle on a reinforced rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handset in its cradle, coiled cord, and two large square buttons beneath the receiver.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the phone, the protruding handset, and the angular side flanges.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a flat, unadorned metal back with visible paint chipping, suitable for wall mounting.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handset profile, coiled cord, and the angular reinforcement on the side.
